title: M3CV: Multi-subject, Multi-session, Multi-task EEG Database
description: M3CV is a multi-subject, multi-session, multi-task EEG database comprising 64-channel recordings from 95 healthy young adults across two sessions. The dataset includes 14 types of EEG signals spanning resting state, motor execution, transient sensory stimulation, steady-state visual/auditory/somatosensory evoked potentials, and P300 oddball paradigms. Originally developed for EEG-based biometric identification and verification challenges, this BIDS-formatted version reconstructs pseudo-continuous EEG from preprocessed epochs, providing a comprehensive resource for brain-computer interface and biometric research.
methods_description: EEG was recorded at 1000 Hz using a 64-channel BrainAmp amplifier with Easycap (10-20 positions), referenced to FCz online and re-referenced to linked mastoids offline. Preprocessing included manual bad channel interpolation, bandpass filtering (0.01-200 Hz), notch filtering (49-51 Hz), ICA-based artifact removal, and downsampling to 250 Hz. Subjects performed 6 paradigms with 15 runs per session (~50 minutes recording per session) including eyes-closed/open resting, motor execution (hand/foot), transient sensory stimulation (visual/auditory/somatosensory evoked potentials), SSVEP variants, and P300 oddball tasks.
